text:
The Kakin Empire (, Kakin Teikoku) is a country in the middle of the Azian Continent, and during the events of the story, becomes one of the countries of the V6. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 346 

History

In the age of rival warlords, Kakin was a small country that was under constant threat of being absorbed by neighboring countries. Around that time, the First King of Kakin conjured the Seed Urn and had his children fight in a succession contest for the throne. Through his children's fight for unification, the foundation for Kakin becoming the present-day superpower that it is was built. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 362 

Ancient Kakin had a practice where princes who failed to become kings were buried together with "afterlife companions", civilians sacrificed in order to supervise the failed princes and prevent them from becoming vengeful ghosts that curse or haunt the king or country. Despite supposedly being a great honor, these "afterlife companions" were always selected from the lowest caste in the system, the Have-Nots. Although the custom of afterlife companions has since been abolished, the caste system, which is immutable, remained, and the Have-Nots remained forbidden from holding public office or holding rank in the military. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Taking advantage of the "Shinrinkan Incident", the country shifted from being a socialist empire to a parliamentary democracy thirty years ago in what was called "The Most Silent Revolution in History". As a result, the name of the country was changed and all treaties considered null by the new government. In recent years, it underwent considerable economic growth. It is currently ruled by Nasubi Hui Guo Rou. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 32, Chapter 340 

Plot

Greed Island arc

Approximately 3 Phantom Troupe members were sent to Kakin when Razor scattered them across the Azian Continent in response to them trespassing on Greed Island. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 15, Chapter 145 

Chimera Ant arc

After using the Greed Island card "Accompany", Gon and Killua are transported out of Greed Island to Kakin where they meet Kite. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 185 Kite had been hired by the Government of Kakin and, together with his team of Amateur Hunters, carries out a biological survey, mainly ecological research, including discovery of new species. Kite's team had been in Kakin for three years, and they still have one month till their contract with Kakin runs out. Although the team had already met their quota, they remain motivated, and Kite invites Gon and Killua to join them in discovering new species. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 186 

The time comes for Kite and the Amateur Hunters to turn in their results from the biological survey to the Kakin government. The zoologist Wong is delighted by their work and claims these results are worth 200 years of their own research, but apologizes for not being able to pay them a bonus. In exchange, he promises to recommend their team to the ministries of other countries, and also casually mentions that the Southernpiece Auction House had acquired a segment of a strange creature. Southernpiece were unable to list it because of its potentially shady legal status, but were hoping to have a licensed Hunter buy it instead of it being confiscated by a bureaucrat. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 18, Chapter 187 

Succession Contest arc

Kakin's King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou announces an expedition to the Dark Continent led by Beyond Netero. The Five Modern Nations initially try to find ways to stop Kakin, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 341 but eventually decide to invite Kakin into their group and reorganize into the V6, in order to save face and effort. The former V5 are content with letting Kakin fake their voyage by ending at a fake "Dark Continent", having their general passengers disembark, and letting the exploration team tempt fate in the true Dark Continent. Kakin unveils the Black Whale, a gigantic ship capable of carrying 200,000 people, as the means of transportation to the Dark Continent, and Nasubi declares he will have 20 more built in the span of a year, as well as to send 100,000,000 people to the Dark Continent in 5 years. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 342 However, the voyage also serves a secret function: it is on the Black Whale 1 that Nasubi plans to decide his successor by having Kakin's fourteen Princes participate in a deathmatch. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 348 

The eve festival was held at a large harbor on the day before the Black Whale 1's departure, emceed by Gantai and Tamazo. The Royal Family and Beyond were introduced before being flown to the Black Whale 1, and they were joined by various VIPs on ferries shortly after, to settle in and to participate in a banquet on the Black Whale 1. The Black Whale 1 set sail for the Dark Continent the next day, and its departure marked the beginning of Kakin's succession contest. 

Government

Royal Family

Kakin is a monarchical empire, ruled by King Nasubi Hui Guo Rou, who has eight wives and fourteen legitimate children.

Queen Unma is the mother of two sons, Benjamin and Tserriednich.
Queen Duazul is the mother of two daughters, Camilla and Tubeppa, and two sons, Luzurus and Halkenburg.
Queen Tang Zhao Li is the mother of one son, Zhang Lei.
Queen Katrono is the mother of one daughter, Tyson.
Queen Swinko-swinko is the mother of one son, Salé-salé.
Queen Seiko is the mother of him two daughters, Kacho and Fugetsu.
Queen Sevanti is the mother of one daughter, Momoze, and one son, Marayam.
Queen Oito is the mother of one daughter, Woble.

Even though there is no official ranking among the wives, there are still some rules and privileges among them. For example, lower-ranking wives cannot contact the higher-ranking ones.

Since gender has no bearing on succession, all the children are referred to as "Prince" — as in the "Nth Prince" in order of birth. All fourteen Princes are currently participating in a succession contest aboard the Black Whale during a voyage to the pretend New Continent, where the sole survivor will be the official heir to the throne. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 349 

Illegitimate children of a king take the name of "Second-Track Fakers" Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 (二線者, Nisenmono) and receive two scars on their face the moment they are born. They are allowed to live Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 and receive ample privileges in the underworld on the condition that they forever remain in the shadows. It is Kakin tradition that a Royal with no right to the Throne becomes the boss of one of the three Mafia Families of the Empire, granting the Royal Family of the Empire power and influence over the Underworld. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 

Regalia

The Kakin Empire is known to have three regalia used in its Succession Contest. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 
The Seed Urn is one of Kakin's three regalia and appears to be a pivotal item for the beginning of a succession contest. Those seeking and qualified to be participants partake in a ceremony to have a Guardian Spirit Beast bestowed upon them. 
The Lotus Anchorite (also described as the overseer of the succession battle) is another of Kakin's three regalia and appears to be what enforces the rules of a succession contest, such as (but not limited to) what happens to those that the boundaries of a succession contest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 
The Sword of Good Omens, the final of Kakin's three regalia, is also described as the Sword of Accession given to the next king, suggesting at a relevance to the ending of a succession contest.

Parliament

Kakin is a parliamentary democracy and has an unknown ruling party. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 345 The real power of these institutions is debatable, however, since King Nasubi seems to be the supreme leader of the country.

Military

The Black Whale operates under martial law for the duration of Kakin's voyage to the "New Continent". The Royal Army has various tasks on board, generally maintaining the peace throughout the ship and managing its operations, including managing all phone lines present in the ship, monitoring the halls, and keeping track of all activities. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 On Tier 1, it acts as a neutral party to the Succession Battle and takes care of miscellaneous tasks like handling the bodies of any deceased. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 The through-ways between the ship's tiers are guarded by the Royal Army. 

Princes' Employees

The Hunter Association restricted each prince to a maximum of fifteen employees on board the Black Whale during their two-month voyage to the pretend New Continent. 

The fifteen employees can range from personal guards (e.g. Maor), Royal Guards/spies from the higher Queens (e.g. Slakka), Royal Guards from the princes' mothers (e.g. Mushaho), servants (e.g. Shimano), to Pro Hunters recruited from outside (e.g. Kurapika) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 350 

Moreover, Princes Camilla, Zhang Lei, Tserriednich, and Tubeppa pushed this limit to 20 by sending 5 of their personal guards to take the 289th Hunter Exam and pass as Provisional Hunters with limited licenses only for the voyage (e.g. Theta). 

Below is a list that specifies the characteristics of these categories:
Personal Guards: Soldiers each prince assembled through their own connections. Only the First Prince's guards also serve as royal army officers. The guards of the oldest five princes have been training since their childhood to give their lives to the prince. Morale and loyalty are high. 
Queen's Guards: Royal army soldiers allotted to each queen. Each prince's guard has little sense of connection to them, but they are loyal to their queens. Some of them are willing to assassinate the prince they are assigned to, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 whereas others abhor the notion and limit themselves to monitoring their prince, making sure he or she does not become a threat to the safety of their client or her children. 
Hunter Association Members: Professional Hunters hired as bodyguards by each individual prince. Exclusively defensive, they do not assist assassinations even by request from the client. 
Provisional Hunters: Each prince's guards and the Hunter Association coordinated to gather personnel to guard areas outside residential quarters. This personnel was given temporary Hunter Association member status as a matter of convenience. Rules have been ignored, and they freely enter quarters. 
Servants: Some also serve as soldiers, and some are non-combatants. The more personnel a prince requests, the more furniture, necessities, and luggage allowed to fit in their residence, and therefore, the fewer weapons they can bring (those with weapons are supplied). 

Queen's Guards

The Royal Guards sent by the higher queens are mostly used as informants (and sometimes assassins) to find out if the lower queens and princes are plotting a rebellion. A queen gets a true Royal Guard only when a lower-ranked queen comes along. Queen Oito, the lowest, had not even had this system explained to her before the departure of the Black Whale. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 360 

To further elaborate on the numbers and divisions of the family guard: 

Neither Prince Benjamin nor Prince Tserriednich have Royal Guards from higher queens since they are the children of the First Queen. 
Additionally, both decided to solely depend on their personal army rather than recruit Royal Guards from their mother's side. However, Benjamin ended up replacing his personal soldiers with Unma's Royal Guards who were spying on the lower princes, in order to gain information of the Hunters' and Guardian Spirit Beasts' abilities of the princes.
It should be noted that Benjamin's personal guards are the only ones who qualify for such task as they are all originally soldiers from the Kakin royal army. 
Likewise, Princes Camilla, Zhang Lei, Tubeppa, Tyson, and Halkenburg decided to solely depend on their personal armies.
Princes Camilla, Tubeppa, Luzurus, and Halkenburg each have one guard chosen by Queen Unma (1 st ). 
Only Prince Luzurus has six guards chosen by his mother Queen Duazul (2 nd ). 
Prince Zhang Lei has two guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) and Duazul (2 nd ).
Prince Tyson has three guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) through Tang Zhao Li (3 rd ).
Prince Salé-salé has four guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) through Katrono (4 th ), and three guards chosen by his mother Queen Swinko-swinko (5 th ).
Both Prince Kacho and Prince Fugetsu have five guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) through Swinko-swinko (5 th ), and two guards chosen by her mother Queen Seiko (6 th ).
Both Prince Momoze and Prince Marayam have six guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) through Seiko (6 th ), and one guard chosen by her mother Queen Sevanti (7 th ). 
Queen Sevanti transferred her second Royal Guard from Momoze to Marayam. 
Prince Woble has seven guards chosen by Queens Unma (1 st ) through Sevanti (7 th ) and none chosen by her mother Queen Oito (8 th ). 

However, it should be noted that the queens are not allowed to order their Royal Guards to assassinate the princes, seeing that even a queen would be imprisoned if she issued such an order. The Royal Guards' job is to still protect the lower princes as long as it does not threaten the safety of their clients and their prince. In fact, even though King Nasubi himself allowed the succession battle, he has promised no acquittal or pardon for murdering a prince. 

 Active/Alive • Deceased • Former/Arrested 

 • • 

Society

The Kakin Empire has a caste society, the lowest caste being the Have-Nots. 

See also: Have-Nots

Lèse-majesté

Certain offenses against the crown are punishable by death. Before modern times, such crimes even included lower-ranking officials speaking up and commoners looking royals in the face. Even after democratization, contraception, abortion, and genetic testing of royals are considered treasonous acts punishable by death. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 

Carne Levare

Once every few years, the royal family travel incognito to a randomly selected village. Every villager is designated as "other" or an "entertainer", in which case they are forced to copulate with the royals with no regard for their own well being, potentially for days on end. Children conceived during a Carne Levare (alternatively Carnival) take the name of "Carnival Orphans" and are separated from their mothers at birth in clinics run by the mafia. They are scarred across the face, and, after being tested, categorized as either "Second-Track Fakers" or "Flesh", the latter designation seemingly condemning the child to a life of awful obligations. 

The latest Carnival was held one year prior to the voyage of the Black Whale, on the same year as the thirtieth anniversary of the country's democratization. 

Calendar

The country uses a calendar that is twenty years (plus or minus some unknown multiple of 100) offset from the main calendar used in the world, as the year before the events of the Succession Contest arc ends in 20 in said calendar, whereas the same year in the main calendar is 2000.

Underworld

The underworld of the Kakin Empire is ruled by three great Mafia families: Xi-Yu, Heil-Ly, and Cha-R. Each of the families is led by the illegitimate child of a Kakin king, and has the support of a rightful heir to the throne. The Kakin mafia boss titles are always inherited by a Second-Track Faker, Kakin's term for a person of royal blood who has no claim to the throne. 

The three mafia families are bound by a code of conduct and traditional rituals that override their rivalry: the nomination of a new boss occurs during an official ceremony that is attended by the bosses of the other two families. At least some of the high-ranking members of the mafia view themselves as supporting Kakin from the shadows, and regard its citizens not as targets, but customers. The preservation of balance is the supreme concern of the mafia, important enough for them to set aside their conflicts to cooperate. 

Members of all three families boarded the Black Whale in order to secure territories and resources in the Dark Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 

Code of Conduct

Prior to Morena's rise to power, the three big Mafia families followed a code of conduct. Members wishing to make a hit or raid on another family tell their bosses in advance, and the bosses contact each other. For example, if family A's underling wishes to launch an attack on family B's underling:

Family A's underling informs family A's boss of his intent to attack family B's underling
Family A's underling attacks family B's underling
Family A's boss contacts family B's boss to discuss compensation

The compensation can come in the form of money, a life, or a limb, but is a chance for the victim family's boss to show his generosity and control. The notification between bosses is often after the fact, but an unwritten expectation that the notification is made within 24 hours exists. The most important matter to ensure is that the boss is away from his home turf when the incident occurs; if a boss was present in their home turf and no notification comes, the incident is treated as an instigation of war, equivalent to personally shooting another boss, continuing until one side is crushed. Members of subordinate families do not get involved in the conflict and do not cooperate with any family in a war. 

During the Black Whale's voyage, the streak of murders attributed to Morena's Heil-Ly family went unanswered for several days, with Morena herself being uncontactable. This would eventually lead to an order being sent for her death by the Xi-Yu, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 390 and is possibly part of the motivation that leads the Cha-R to request the Heil-Ly crushed. 

On the Black Whale

The three Mafia families each control one of the lower three tiers of the boat. The bosses reside in Tier 1, with apartments as luxurious as the upper-ranked queens and luggage capacity second only to the king's, for as long as they obey royal authority. Onior and Brocco dwell in Tier 1, while Morena's resides in the Heil-Ly secret base between Tiers 2 and 3 with her underlings. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

Trivia

In Chapter 361, the cap of a Kakin Army's soldier seemingly has the word "Kakin", in a stylized way, on it, confirming the in-universe name of the nation. 
Contradictorily, in Chapter 371, the stylized inscription on Cleapatro's room and the parts shown the word on their clothing indicate the word "Cakin"—also a possible in-story spelling of the country's name.
The term used to refer to illegitimate children of a Kakin king is "Nisenmono" (二線者), a neologism derived from the Japanese "nisenkyu" (二線級, "secondary/inferior") and "nisemono" (偽物, "fake"). Possible English counterparts would be "Second Raker" or "Fakondary Princes".
It is extremely rude and disrespectful for a commoner to abbreviate the name of the Royal Family members. 

Intertextuality and References

The three Mafia families seem to be based off triads, while also forming one in the literal sense of "group of three".
